steps.title: Set the equations equal and solve for x
steps.content: Since both expressions equal $y$, set them equal to each other:$$3x + 1 = 4x - 6$$Solve for $x$ by collecting like terms:$$3x - 4x = -6 - 1$$$$-x = -7$$$$x = 7$$

steps.title: Substitute x into an original equation to find y
steps.content: Use $y = 3x + 1$ with $x = 7$:$$y = 3(7) + 1 = 21 + 1 = 22$$The intersection point is $(7, 22)$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
